question_text: A compass needle is placed near a bar magnet with the magnet's north pole pointing east. Which direction will the compass needle point?

options.label: A
options.text: The needle will point west, away from the magnet's north pole.

options.label: B
options.text: The needle will point east, towards the magnet's north pole.

options.label: C
options.text: The needle will stop moving because the compass is broken.

options.label: D
options.text: The needle will point north, following Earth's magnetic field.

explanation: The compass needle's north pole is repelled by the magnet's north pole, so it rotates to point away (west). Remember: like poles repel, so the north-seeking end of the compass needle points away from the magnet's north pole.
